symptoms.symptom_name: Severe burning retrosternal chest pain waking the patient from sleep

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nocturnal burning retrosternal pain is typical of reflux, but is non-specific and shared with cardiac pain.
Severe chest pain waking a patient from sleep is a high-risk feature mandating cardiac exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset after a large, late, high-fat meal with alcohol

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Large late fatty meals and alcohol lower LOS tone and delay gastric emptying — classic reflux trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acid regurgitation into mouth/throat with water brash

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acid regurgitation and water brash are specific reflux symptoms and are not features of cardiac ischaemia.
Acid regurgitation shifts probability toward reflux but does not exclude coexisting cardiac disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest discomfort worse supine and on bending, eased by sitting up

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positional (postural) provocation by recumbency is characteristic of acid reflux via loss of gravitational clearance.
Positional pain is less typical of ischaemia but does not exclude it.
Antacid response is consistent with an acid-related cause but is NOT reliable to exclude cardiac pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nocturnal dry cough and morning throat-clearing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Extra-oesophageal reflux features (nocturnal cough, throat-clearing) from micro-aspiration/laryngeal irritation.

symptoms.symptom_name: No diaphoresis, no radiation to arm/jaw, no breathlessness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of these features lowers cardiac probability but cannot exclude ACS — history is not a substitute for an ECG.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal 12-lead ECG during active pain (no ST/T ischaemic changes)

investigation_details.expected_result: Normal sinus rhythm, no ST-segment or T-wave ischaemic abnormality
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Acute coronary syndrome cannot be excluded; the whole basis for a benign attribution is missing.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal ECG during pain (with clinical context) is the discriminator that excluded ischaemia in person — unavailable via telehealth, hence sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild epigastric tenderness on deep palpation, no guarding or rebound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild epigastric tenderness is consistent with acid-related upper-GI disease; requires hands-on examination, so sealed.
Epigastric tenderness can accompany peptic ulcer disease — a reasonable alternative acid-related cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals: BP 140/85, HR 95, RR 16, SpO2 99%, afebrile

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mildly raised BP/HR are consistent with anxiety/pain; non-specific. Clinician-measured, so sealed — the AI infers distress from the patient's report, not a measured vital.
Raised body weight/central adiposity increases intra-abdominal pressure and is a recognised reflux risk factor (also a cardiovascular risk factor).

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Retrosternal burning + acid regurgitation + worse supine + antacid relief together strongly favour reflux — but do not, on their own, exclude a cardiac cause of the chest pain.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Primary discriminator to exclude acute coronary syndrome in a patient with severe nocturnal chest pain; must be arranged in person as it cannot be done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum troponin (in person, if cardiac suspicion persists)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Complements the ECG for ACS exclusion when chest pain is significant; requires venous bloods, unavailable via telehealth.
Not indicated acutely for uncomplicated reflux; reserve for alarm features (dysphagia, GI bleeding, weight loss, anaemia) or PPI-refractory symptoms — do not over-order.
Reasonable in follow-up for acid-related upper-GI symptoms, but not an acute-episode priority.
